message:
fix: improve test reliability and guard hook advisory mode forwarding

This commit fixes several issues related to test robustness, API compatibility,
and guard hook behavior when advisory mode is enabled.

Major changes:

1. Guard hook advisory flag forwarding (guard.py):
   - FIXED: Pre-commit and pre-push hooks now properly forward ADVISORY mode to CLI
   - Before: Hooks always invoked `guard check` without --advisory flag
   - After: When ADVISORY=True (AGENT_MAIL_GUARD_MODE=warn), hooks append --advisory to argv
   - Both uv and python fallback paths now respect advisory mode
   - This ensures consistent behavior: warn mode doesn't block at ANY level

   Implementation:
   ```python
   argv = ["uv","run","python","-m","mcp_agent_mail.cli","guard","check","--stdin-nul","--repo", root]
   if ADVISORY:
       argv.append("--advisory")
   proc = subprocess.run(argv, input=payload, check=False)
   ```

   Why this matters:
   - Before: Setting AGENT_MAIL_GUARD_MODE=warn would print warnings in hook but still exit 1
   - After: Advisory mode properly exits 0 at CLI level, hook doesn't re-check exit code
   - Users can now use warn mode for dry-run conflict detection without blocking commits

2. Context logging safety (app.py):
   - NEW: _ctx_info_safe() helper that catches exceptions when context unavailable
   - Replaces direct ctx.info() calls in ensure_project and guard install/uninstall tools
   - Prevents crashes when tools are called from tests or non-request contexts
   - Gracefully degrades: logging is best-effort, not critical to tool functionality

   Why this matters:
   - FastMCP Context objects may not be available in all invocation paths
   - Tests that call tools directly (via _mcp_call_tool) don't have request context
   - Crashing on ctx.info() is a poor developer experience
   - Silent fallback maintains tool functionality while losing non-critical logging

3. E2E test improvements (tests/integration/test_worktrees_functionality_e2e.py):
   - FIXED: Uses FastMCP public async API instead of accessing internal _tools/_resources
   - Before: Iterated getattr(mcp, "_tools", []) which is fragile across FastMCP versions
   - After: Calls mcp.get_tools() and mcp.get_resources() (proper async getters)
   - Before: Called tool.run(args) directly on FunctionTool
   - After: Calls mcp._mcp_call_tool(name, args) which handles serialization correctly
   - Before: Called resource.func(key) directly
   - After: Calls mcp._mcp_read_resource(uri) which returns proper ResourceContent objects

   Test setup improvements:
   - Sets STORAGE_ROOT environment variable for consistent archive location
   - Creates file reservation AFTER obtaining project slug (proper ordering)
   - Uses install_precommit_guard MCP tool instead of CLI subprocess
   - Adds unique suffix to product names to avoid cross-run collisions
   - Handles nested event loop via threading (robust async execution)

   Resource reading fix:
   ```python
   # Before (fragile):
   res = next(r for r in mcp._resources if r.name == "resource://product/{key}")
   payload = res.func(prod["product_uid"])

   # After (robust):
   res_list = await mcp._mcp_read_resource(f"resource://product/{prod['product_uid']}")
   payload = json.loads(res_list[0].content)
   ```

4. Product Bus unit test improvements (tests/test_product_bus.py):
   - Enables WORKTREES_ENABLED=1 via monkeypatch (gate enforcement)
   - Clears settings cache before test to ensure fresh configuration
   - Uses unique product key to avoid collisions across test runs
   - Uses mcp._mcp_call_tool instead of accessing FunctionTool internals
   - Uses mcp._mcp_read_resource for reading resource://product
   - Properly deserializes JSON from ResourceContent.content field

5. Documentation clarifications (AGENTS.md, README.md, PLAN):
   - Added "(available only when WORKTREES_ENABLED=1)" to resource://identity/{path}
   - Makes it clear that identity resource is gated like Product Bus tools
   - Consistent messaging across all documentation files

Why this matters:

Advisory mode bug fix:
- This was a significant UX issue: users setting warn mode expected non-blocking behavior
- Before fix: hooks would warn but still exit 1, blocking commits despite "advisory" label
- After fix: proper end-to-end advisory mode (warn + exit 0)
- Critical for gradual rollout: teams can enable guards in warn-only mode first

Test reliability:
- Before: Tests were brittle, accessing FastMCP internals that could change
- After: Tests use proper async APIs that are part of FastMCP's contract
- Reduces maintenance burden when FastMCP updates
- Avoids "test works locally but fails in CI" scenarios

Context safety:
- Before: Tool calls from tests could crash on ctx.info()
- After: Logging is optional, tools work in all contexts
- Improves developer experience when writing integration tests
- Follows "fail gracefully" principle

Test isolation:
- Unique product names prevent test pollution across runs
- STORAGE_ROOT set explicitly avoids default location conflicts
- Proper gate enabling ensures tests reflect production behavior

API compatibility:
- _mcp_call_tool handles argument validation and serialization
- _mcp_read_resource returns proper ResourceContent with mime types
- Future FastMCP changes to internal structures won't break our tests

Backward compatibility:
- Hook script regeneration required to get advisory flag forwarding fix
- Old hooks continue to work (just won't properly forward advisory mode)
- Context safety is transparent (no user-visible changes)
- Test changes don't affect production behavior

Testing:
- Run E2E test: pytest tests/integration/test_worktrees_functionality_e2e.py -v -s
- Verify advisory mode: AGENT_MAIL_GUARD_MODE=warn AGENT_NAME=Me git commit (should warn, not block)
- Product Bus test: pytest tests/test_product_bus.py -v
- All tests should pass with unique product IDs and proper API usage

🤖 Generated with Claude Code (https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 - Install guards (chain-runner friendly; honors `core.hooksPath` and Husky):
   - `mcp-agent-mail guard status .`
   - `mcp-agent-mail guard install <project_key> . --prepush`

-    res = resource.func(prod["product_uid"])  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
-    assert any(p["slug"] == slug for p in res["projects"])

+    payload = json.loads(res_list[0].content)
+    assert any(p["slug"] == slug for p in payload.get("projects", []))
